notes: I was able to put most settings on ultra. For some strange reason 8x anti-aliasing would not work. I set it to 4x and the game now runs perfectly.

title: Company of Heroes

notes:
Single player skirmish games work well.

Multiplayer connects and works initially but after some time (hard to tell how long) the connection silently fails. With no reported loss of connectivity between players the game seems to continue but the AI must take over because I have confirmed that I am no longer playing the same game as my opponent.

Graphics are fine except that the Cinnamon toolbar shows at the bottom of the screen when the game is supposed to be full screen. This doesn't really effect game play.
title: Company of Heroes

notes: Excellent Performance. Single Player and Online work without any issues.

title: Company of Heroes

notes: I had to launch it with `PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%` to get it to stop crashing during the in-game benchmark. Raising the graphical settigns too high caused it to start crashing again.
title: Company of Heroes

notes:
First: Disable D3d11 with with launch parameter "PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%". After this tweak the game ran pretty good for me. expect some crashes between campaing missons, but this was not a big deal cause my progress was saved.

Tested: Campaing & skimirsh. Have no clue about Multiplayer.
title: Company of Heroes

notes: After setting PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% in the launch options, the game run flawlessly. Before doing it, it would randomly crash when loading a mission or in the middle of a session. Afterwards, no issues whatsoever.
title: Company of Heroes

notes: Note: Only worked when game data is stored on a linux (ext4) partition. NTFS did not work for me, game didn't even launch
title: Company of Heroes

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
title: Company of Heroes

notes: Game crashes often when running benchmark. loading missions and in mission on cutscenes. Disabling esync, fsync d3d11 and enabling Large Address Aware fixes theese issues. I ended up with the following startoptions: "PROTON_USE_D9VK=0 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PROTON_NO_FSYNC=1 %command% -notriplebuffer -refresh 60"
title: Company of Heroes

notes: Crashed once on multiplayer game launch, and once after finishing a multiplayer game.  But far more stable than I was used to under native Win7 so no complaints!
title: Company of Heroes

notes: Runs great via d9vk. Game crashes after loading a map if d3d11 is enabled
